title: BUG: use `CMPLX()`/`CMPLXF()` instead of `real + imag * I`
author: devdanzin
body:
#### Reference issue

See gh-24927. Follow-up to gh-24826 which fixed the same bug in `_complexstuff.h`.

#### What does this implement/fix?

The `real + imag * I` pattern corrupts the real part when the imaginary part is NaN, because `NaN * I` (i.e., `NaN * (0.0 + 1.0i)`) produces `NaN + NaN*i` — the multiplication by the real component of `I` (which is 0.0) turns `NaN * 0.0` into NaN, destroying the original real part. The C11 `CMPLX`/`CMPLXF` macros construct the complex number directly without multiplication, correctly handling NaN/Inf.

gh-24826 fixed this in `_complexstuff.h`. These four remaining sites use the identical pattern but were not addressed:

- `ARNAUD_cplx`/`ARNAUD_cplxf` in `arnaud/types.h` (ARPACK eigenvalue solver)
- `PROPACK_cplx`/`PROPACK_cplxf` in `PROPACK/types.h` (SVD solver)
- `ZVODE_cplx` in `blaslapack_declarations.h` (ODE integrator)
- `CPLX_Z`/`CPLX_C` in `_common_array_utils.h` (linalg utilities)

NaN intermediates are normal during eigenvalue/SVD convergence iterations, so these macros can be reached with NaN arguments in production use. The MSVC paths already used `_Cbuild`/`_FCbuild`/`_Dcomplex{...}` which handle special values correctly — this change makes the non-MSVC paths match.

#### Additional information

Seven lines changed across four files. All four files already `#include <complex.h>` which provides `CMPLX`/`CMPLXF` on C11 compilers.

Found by [cext-review-toolkit](https://github.com/devdanzin/cext-review-toolkit).

#### AI Generation Disclosure

Claude Opus 4.6 was used to identify these remaining sites during a systematic C extension analysis using the cext-review-toolkit plugin (the same analysis that led to gh-24826), and to prepare this PR. The fix was confirmed correct by human review.

comments.author: devdanzin
comments.body:
Thanks for the review!

I've extracted the `#ifdef` fallbacks into the suggested shared header at `scipy/_build_utils/src/scipy_complex_support.h` and updated the five files to use it.

The shared header provides both `CMPLX` and `CMPLXF` with the same fallback chain from gh-24826:
1. Compiler-provided C11 `CMPLX`/`CMPLXF` (if already defined)
2. MSVC `_Cbuild`/`_FCbuild`
3. `__builtin_complex` (GCC/Clang)
4. Union type-pun (portable last resort)

`CMPLXF` is needed by 3 of the 4 files for their single-precision complex macros. gh-24826 didn't need it since `_complexstuff.h` only uses double.

`_complexstuff.h` now includes this header, and the 4 previously modified files had their `#include <complex.h>` replaced with `#include "scipy_complex_support.h"` (which includes `<complex.h>`). The MSVC branches in each file are left as before since they already handle NaN/Inf correctly IIUC.

Does this seem reasonable?

comments.author: ev-br
comments.body:
CI errors look related:

```
../scipy/sparse/linalg/_eigen/arpack/_arpackmodule.c:14:13: error: 'ARNAUD_cplx' macro redefined [-Werror,-Wmacro-redefined]
   14 |     #define ARNAUD_cplx(real, imag) ((real) + (imag)*I)
      |             ^
../scipy/sparse/linalg/_eigen/arpack/arnaud/include/arnaud/types.h:27:13: note: previous definition is here
   27 |     #define ARNAUD_cplx(real, imag) CMPLX(real, imag)
      |             ^
../scipy/sparse/linalg/_eigen/arpack/_arpackmodule.c:15:13: error: 'ARNAUD_cplxf' macro redefined [-Werror,-Wmacro-redefined]
   15 |     #define ARNAUD_cplxf(real, imag) ((real) + (imag)*I)
      |             ^
../scipy/sparse/linalg/_eigen/arpack/arnaud/include/arnaud/types.h:26:13: note: previous definition is here
   26 |     #define ARNAUD_cplxf(real, imag) CMPLXF(real, imag)
```

comments.author: devdanzin
comments.body:
I missed that, thank you!

`_arpackmodule.c` had its own copy of the `ARNAUD_cplx`/`ARNAUD_cplxf` macro definitions with the old buggy `(real) + (imag)*I` pattern. This PR fixed `types.h` to use `CMPLX`/`CMPLXF`, but since `_arpackmodule.c` includes `arnaud.h` -> `types.h`, both definitions were now active, causing the macro redefinition error. The fix removes the redundant definitions from `_arpackmodule.c`.

Does that look correct?

comments.author: rgommers
comments.body: Not quite - the `arnoud` sources are meant to be usable independent of SciPy. So rather than include a scipy header, can you add the plain C `CMPLX` definition right inside `arnaud/types.h`? The duplication is fine here.

comments.author: lucascolley
comments.body:
> Not quite - the `arnoud` sources are meant to be usable independent of SciPy. So rather than include a scipy header, can you add the plain C `CMPLX` definition right inside `arnaud/types.h`? The duplication is fine here.

It sounds silly because the file is so small, but in an ideal world, would `scipy_complex_stuff.h` be a separate tiny header-only library that could be packaged and distributed independently? I would imagine this is something that is useful much more broadly than just for SciPy?

comments.author: ilayn
comments.body: That would be a bit like the [`isEven.js`](https://www.npmjs.com/package/is-even). This is just dealing with different compilers not every project needs it unless they want to distribute code. And resolves back to a single line. It is an unfortunate state of affairs with the C99 compatibility mostly Microsoft (surprise).

comments.author: devdanzin
comments.body: Is this better @rgommers? Should we also consider PROPACK to be usable independent of SciPy?

comments.author: lucascolley
comments.body:
> Should we also consider PROPACK to be usable independent of SciPy?

I believe the answer is no given https://github.com/scipy/scipy/pull/24844#issuecomment-4068970981, although not sure if @ilayn responded with thoughts on that anywhere.
